title: GetResponse vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for your needs.
intro: GetResponse and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they cater to different audiences. GetResponse positions itself as a complete marketing suite for course creators and webinar hosts, while Omnisend specializes in ecommerce with built-in SMS and omnichannel automation. This comparison helps you choose the right tool for your business.
choose_a_if: You're creating online courses or hosting webinars and need landing pages and funnels
You want a larger free tier with up to 500 subscribers before upgrading
You prefer a single platform with conversion funnels and course hosting built-in
choose_b_if: You run an ecommerce business and want SMS and email marketing combined
You need omnichannel automation with push notifications and advanced segmentation
You prioritize customer satisfaction (higher Trustpilot ratings) and competitive pricing

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Omnisend has higher customer ratings (4.6 vs 3.7 on Trustpilot) and is better for ecommerce with SMS capabilities. GetResponse is more versatile for course creators and offers a larger free tier, making it better for non-ecommerce businesses.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend starts at $16/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month. However, GetResponse offers 500 free subscribers versus Omnisend's 250, so the free tier is more generous with GetResponse. Your actual costs depend on subscriber count and features needed.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your contact lists and email data. Most migrations are straightforward, though you'll need to rebuild automations and workflows in your new platform. Consider planning the migration during a low-traffic period.
